title_en: Circular Motion — Centripetal Acceleration and Force
title_he: תנועה מעגלית — תאוצה ריכוזית וכוח ריכוזי
subject: physics

sections.body_en_md:
A car turning a corner, a satellite orbiting Earth, a ball on a string — all move in circular paths. Despite constant speed, they are **always accelerating** because their direction changes. This centripetal acceleration requires a net force directed toward the center.

**Bagrut exam topics:**
- Centripetal acceleration and force
- Period, frequency, angular velocity
- Banking roads, satellites, roller coasters
sections.body_he_md:
מכונית בפנייה, לוויין בסביבת כדור הארץ, כדור על חוט — כולם נעים במסלול מעגלי. למרות מהירות קבועה, הם **תמיד מאיצים** כי כיוונם משתנה.

**נושאי בגרות:**
- תאוצה ריכוזית וכוח ריכוזי
- מחזור, תדר, מהירות זוויתית
- כביש מוטה, לוויינים, מתקני שעשועים

sections.body_en_md:
**Angular velocity:** $\omega = 2\pi f = 2\pi/T$ [rad/s]

**Linear speed from angular:** $v = r\omega$

**Centripetal acceleration** (directed toward center):
$$a_c = \frac{v^2}{r} = \omega^2 r$$

**Centripetal force** (net force toward center):
$$F_c = \frac{mv^2}{r} = m\omega^2 r$$

**Period:** $T = 1/f = 2\pi r/v$

Note: "Centripetal force" is NOT a new force — it is the NET force (or component of net force) directed toward the center, provided by real forces (tension, friction, gravity, normal).
sections.body_he_md:
**מהירות זוויתית:** $\omega = 2\pi f = 2\pi/T$ [rad/s]

**מהירות לינארית:** $v = r\omega$

**תאוצה ריכוזית:** $a_c = v^2/r = \omega^2 r$ (לכיוון המרכז)

**כוח ריכוזי:** $F_c = mv^2/r = m\omega^2 r$

**הערה:** "כוח ריכוזי" אינו כוח חדש — זהו הכוח הנטו לכיוון המרכז.

sections.body_en_md:
### Why is there acceleration in uniform circular motion?
Velocity is a vector. Even at constant speed, the direction of $\vec{v}$ changes continuously. By definition, $a = \Delta v / \Delta t$; a changing direction means $\vec{a} \neq 0$.

### Derivation
In time $\Delta t$, the position rotates by $\Delta\theta$, and $|\Delta\vec{v}| = v\Delta\theta$. 
$$a = \frac{|\Delta\vec{v}|}{\Delta t} = v\frac{\Delta\theta}{\Delta t} = v\omega = \frac{v^2}{r}$$

Direction: toward the center (centripetal = "center-seeking").

### Special cases
- **Banked road** (no friction): $\tan\theta = v^2/(rg)$
- **Satellite** (circular orbit): $g = v^2/r \Rightarrow v_{\text{orbit}} = \sqrt{gR}$
- **Vertical loop minimum speed** (at top): $mg = mv^2/r \Rightarrow v_{\min} = \sqrt{gr}$
sections.body_he_md:
### מדוע יש תאוצה בתנועה מעגלית אחידה?
מהירות היא וקטור — שינוי כיוון משמעו $\vec{a} \neq 0$.

### גזירה
$|\Delta\vec{v}| = v\Delta\theta \Rightarrow a = v\omega = v^2/r$

### מקרים מיוחדים
- **כביש מוטה** (ללא חיכוך): $\tan\theta = v^2/(rg)$
- **לוויין**: $v = \sqrt{gR}$
- **לולאה אנכית** (ראש הלולאה): $v_{\min} = \sqrt{gr}$

sections.body_en_md:
**Given:** A $0.5\;\text{kg}$ ball is swung in a horizontal circle of radius $r = 1.2\;\text{m}$ at $v = 4\;\text{m/s}$. Find the tension in the string.

$$F_c = T = \frac{mv^2}{r} = \frac{0.5\times16}{1.2} = \frac{8}{1.2} \approx 6.67\;\text{N}$$
A $1000\;\text{kg}$ car drives over a hill of radius $R = 50\;\text{m}$ at $v = 20\;\text{m/s}$. Find the normal force at the top of the hill ($g = 10$).
sections.body_he_md: מכונית $1000\;\text{kg}$ עולה על גבעה רדיוס $R = 50\;\text{m}$ ב-$v = 20\;\text{m/s}$. מצא כוח נורמלי בראש הגבעה.
sections.checkpoint_solution_en: At top of hill, $mg - N = mv^2/R$ (centripetal force directed down = toward center). $N = m(g - v^2/R) = 1000(10 - 400/50) = 1000(10-8) = 2000\;\text{N}$.

sections.body_en_md:
**Given:** A road is banked at $\theta = 20°$. Find the ideal speed (no friction needed) for a turn of radius $r = 80\;\text{m}$.

**Analysis:** Forces are normal force $N$ (perpendicular to road) and gravity $mg$. Centripetal direction = horizontal toward center.

$$N\sin\theta = \frac{mv^2}{r} \quad \text{(horizontal)}$$
$$N\cos\theta = mg \quad \text{(vertical)}$$

Dividing:
$$\tan\theta = \frac{v^2}{rg} \Rightarrow v = \sqrt{rg\tan\theta}$$
$$v = \sqrt{80\times10\times\tan20°} = \sqrt{800\times0.364} = \sqrt{291.2} \approx 17.1\;\text{m/s}$$

The ISS orbits Earth at height $h = 400\;\text{km}$ above the surface. Earth radius $R_E = 6400\;\text{km}$, $g_{\text{surface}} = 10\;\text{m/s}^2$. Estimate orbital speed (assuming $g$ doesn't change much).
sections.body_he_md: ה-ISS מקיף בגובה $h = 400\;\text{km}$. $R_E = 6400\;\text{km}$, $g = 10\;\text{m/s}^2$. הערך מהירות מסלולית (הנח $g$ קבוע).

sections.body_en_md:
**Given:** A $0.3\;\text{kg}$ ball on a $0.8\;\text{m}$ string moves in a vertical circle. Find the minimum speed at the top and the tension at the bottom when moving at minimum speed at the top.

**(a) Minimum speed at top:**
At top, minimum speed when tension = 0: $mg = mv^2_{\text{top}}/r$
$$v_{\text{top,min}} = \sqrt{gr} = \sqrt{10\times0.8} = \sqrt{8} \approx 2.83\;\text{m/s}$$

**(b) Speed at bottom** (using energy conservation):
$$\frac{1}{2}mv_{\text{bot}}^2 = \frac{1}{2}mv_{\text{top}}^2 + mg(2r)$$
$$v_{\text{bot}}^2 = v_{\text{top}}^2 + 4gr = 8 + 4(10)(0.8) = 8 + 32 = 40$$

**(c) Tension at bottom:**
$$T - mg = \frac{mv_{\text{bot}}^2}{r}$$
$$T = m\left(g + \frac{v_{\text{bot}}^2}{r}\right) = 0.3\left(10 + \frac{40}{0.8}\right) = 0.3(10+50) = 18\;\text{N}$$

| Situation | Net force toward center | Key equation |
|---|---|---|
| Ball on string (horizontal) | Tension | $T = mv^2/r$ |
| Car on flat road (turn) | Friction | $f = mv^2/r$ |
| Top of hill | $mg - N$ | $N = m(g - v^2/r)$ |
| Bottom of valley | $N - mg$ | $N = m(g + v^2/r)$ |
| Banked road (no friction) | $N\sin\theta$ | $\tan\theta = v^2/(rg)$ |
| Satellite | Gravity | $g = v^2/r$ → $v = \sqrt{gr}$ |
| Vertical loop: top | $mg + T$ | $v_{\min} = \sqrt{gr}$ at $T=0$ |
sections.body_he_md:
| מצב | כוח ריכוזי | נוסחה |
|---|---|---|
| כדור על חוט | מתח | $T = mv^2/r$ |
| פנייה בכביש ישר | חיכוך | $f = mv^2/r$ |
| ראש גבעה | $mg - N$ | $N = m(g-v^2/r)$ |
| כביש מוטה | $N\sin\theta$ | $\tan\theta = v^2/(rg)$ |
| לוויין | כבידה | $v = \sqrt{gr}$ |

exercises.body_en: A $2\;\text{kg}$ object moves in a circle of radius $r = 0.5\;\text{m}$ at $v = 3\;\text{m/s}$. Find centripetal force.
An object moves in a circle of radius $r = 2\;\text{m}$ with period $T = 4\;\text{s}$. Find $v$, $\omega$, and $a_c$.
A car travels at $v = 20\;\text{m/s}$ around a circular track of radius $r = 100\;\text{m}$. Find the centripetal acceleration.
The Moon orbits Earth at radius $r = 3.8\times10^8\;\text{m}$ with period $T = 27.3\;\text{days}$. Find the orbital speed.
exercises.body_he: הירח מקיף ב-$r = 3.8\times10^8\;\text{m}$, $T = 27.3$ ימים. מצא מהירות מסלולית.

exercises.body_en: A $800\;\text{kg}$ car takes a turn of radius $r = 60\;\text{m}$ on a flat road. Maximum friction coefficient $\mu_s = 0.5$. Find maximum safe speed.
A roller coaster loop has radius $r = 10\;\text{m}$. Find (a) minimum speed at top, (b) normal force at bottom when speed = $20\;\text{m/s}$, $m = 500\;\text{kg}$.
exercises.body_he: לולאת מתקן שעשועים $r = 10\;\text{m}$, $m = 500\;\text{kg}$. (א) מהירות מינימלית בראש, (ב) $N$ בתחתית עם $v = 20\;\text{m/s}$.

exercises.body_en: A road is banked at $\theta = 15°$ for a curve of radius $r = 120\;\text{m}$. Find the ideal speed.
A satellite orbits Earth at altitude $h = 200\;\text{km}$. Earth radius $R_E = 6400\;\text{km}$, $g = 9.8\;\text{m/s}^2$. Find orbital speed and period.
A ball on a string ($r = 0.8\;\text{m}$, $m = 0.2\;\text{kg}$) swings in a conical pendulum (string makes angle $\theta = 30°$ with vertical). Find speed and period.
exercises.body_he: מטוטלת חרוטית: חוט $0.8\;\text{m}$, $m = 0.2\;\text{kg}$, זווית $\theta = 30°$ מהאנכי. מצא מהירות ומחזור.
exercises.solution_en: Radius of circle: $r = L\sin30° = 0.4\;\text{m}$. $T\cos30° = mg \Rightarrow T = mg/\cos30° = 2/(0.866) = 2.31\;\text{N}$. $T\sin30° = mv^2/r$: $v^2 = Tr\sin30°/m = 2.31(0.4)(0.5)/0.2 = 2.31$. $v \approx 1.52\;\text{m/s}$. $T_{\text{period}} = 2\pi r/v = 2\pi(0.4)/1.52 \approx 1.65\;\text{s}$.

exercises.body_en: A car ($m = 1200\;\text{kg}$) drives over a bridge modeled as a circular arc ($r = 30\;\text{m}$). Find the maximum speed before the car leaves the road (normal force = 0).
exercises.body_he: מכונית ($m = 1200\;\text{kg}$) עוברת גשר-קשת ($r = 30\;\text{m}$). מהירות מקסימלית לפני ניתוק ($N = 0$).

exercises.body_en: A $50\;\text{g}$ ball is attached to a $1\;\text{m}$ string and moves in a vertical circle. At the top, the string barely stays taut (tension = 0). Find the speed and tension at the side (at the 3 o'clock position).
exercises.body_he: כדור $50\;\text{g}$, חוט $1\;\text{m}$, לולאה אנכית. בראש החוט דק בדיוק (מתח = 0). מצא מהירות ומתח ב"3 בשעון".
exercises.solution_en: At top: $v_{\text{top}} = \sqrt{gr} = \sqrt{10} \approx 3.16\;\text{m/s}$. Side is $r = 1\;\text{m}$ below top (height drop = $r$). Energy: $v_{\text{side}}^2 = v_{\text{top}}^2 + 2gr = 10 + 20 = 30$. At side (3 o'clock), centripetal is horizontal, tension provides centripetal: $T = mv_{\text{side}}^2/r = 0.05(30)/1 = 1.5\;\text{N}$.

exercises.body_en: A test pilot pulls out of a dive in a circular arc of radius $r = 600\;\text{m}$ at speed $v = 300\;\text{m/s}$ at the bottom. Find the apparent weight as a multiple of $g$ ($\text{g-force}$).
exercises.body_he: טייס יוצא מצלילה בקשת $r = 600\;\text{m}$ ב-$v = 300\;\text{m/s}$. מצא משקל ממשי כמכפלה של $g$.

exercises.body_en: Derive the formula for the ideal banking angle $\tan\theta = v^2/(rg)$ from first principles.
exercises.body_he: גזור את נוסחת הנטייה האידיאלית $\tan\theta = v^2/(rg)$ מעקרונות ראשוניים.
exercises.solution_en: On a banked road, the forces are: gravity ($mg$ down) and normal force $N$ (perpendicular to road surface). No friction. Vertical: $N\cos\theta = mg$. Horizontal (centripetal): $N\sin\theta = mv^2/r$. Divide: $\tan\theta = (mv^2/r)/(mg) = v^2/(rg)$ ∎
exercises.solution_he: ניתוח כוחות: $N\cos\theta = mg$; $N\sin\theta = mv^2/r$. חלוקה: $\tan\theta = v^2/(rg)$ ∎

sections.body_en_md:
1. **"Centrifugal force" is not real.** In an inertial frame, there is only the centripetal force (toward center). "Centrifugal" is a fictitious force felt in a rotating reference frame.

2. **Centripetal force is not a new force.** It is always provided by existing forces (tension, friction, gravity, normal).

3. **Speed can be constant but acceleration is not zero.** In UCM, $|v|$ = const but $\vec{v}$ changes direction continuously.

4. **At top of loop:** The centripetal direction is DOWNWARD (toward center). Both $mg$ and $T$ act downward: $mg + T = mv^2/r$.
sections.body_he_md:
1. **"כוח צנטריפוגלי" אינו אמיתי** — במסגרת אינרציאלית אין כוח כזה.

2. **הכוח הריכוזי אינו כוח חדש** — הוא תמיד מסופק על-ידי כוחות קיימים.

3. **מהירות קבועה ≠ ללא תאוצה** — הכיוון משתנה.

4. **בראש הלולאה:** מרכז למטה → $mg + T = mv^2/r$.

sections.body_en_md:
- $a_c = v^2/r = \omega^2 r$; $F_c = mv^2/r$
- $v = r\omega$; $\omega = 2\pi f = 2\pi/T$
- Draw FBD, identify centripetal direction, set net force = $mv^2/r$
- Banked: $\tan\theta = v^2/(rg)$
- Loop top (min): $v_{\min} = \sqrt{gr}$ when $T = 0$
- Satellite: $v = \sqrt{gR}$
sections.body_he_md:
- $a_c = v^2/r$; $F_c = mv^2/r$
- $v = r\omega$; $\omega = 2\pi/T$
- כביש מוטה: $\tan\theta = v^2/(rg)$
- לולאה ראש: $v_{\min} = \sqrt{gr}$
- לוויין: $v = \sqrt{gR}$

sections.body_en_md:
- **Centripetal acceleration:** $a_c = v^2/r = \omega^2 r$ (toward center)
- **Centripetal force:** net force = $mv^2/r$ (toward center, provided by tension/friction/gravity)
- **Uniform circular motion:** speed constant, direction changing continuously
- **Banked road:** $\tan\theta = v^2/(rg)$
- **Satellite:** $v = \sqrt{gR}$
sections.body_he_md:
- $a_c = v^2/r$; $F_c = mv^2/r$
- תנועה מעגלית אחידה: $|v|$ קבוע, כיוון משתנה
- כביש מוטה: $\tan\theta = v^2/(rg)$
- לוויין: $v = \sqrt{gR}$

questions.stem_en: What provides the centripetal force for a car rounding a flat curve?
questions.stem_he: מה מספק את הכוח הריכוזי למכונית בפנייה שטוחה?
questions.explanation_en: On a flat road, the centripetal direction is horizontal. Only friction can provide a horizontal force toward the center.
questions.explanation_he: בכביש שטוח, הכיוון הריכוזי הוא אופקי — רק חיכוך יכול לספק כוח אופקי.

key_insights: Centripetal acceleration: $a_c = v^2/r$, always toward center.
Centripetal force is NOT a new force — identify which real forces provide it.
Uniform circular motion: speed constant, velocity direction changes.
Key cases: flat turn (friction), banked turn, loop-the-loop, satellite.

common_misconceptions.wrong: There is a centrifugal force in circular motion
common_misconceptions.correction: Centrifugal force is fictitious (only felt in rotating reference frame). In inertial frame: centripetal force (toward center) is all there is.

agent_hints.tutor_pacing_hint: Start with the concept: direction changes → acceleration. Then $a_c = v^2/r$. Then identify the real force that provides it case by case. Banking and satellites are harder — always draw FBD.
